title: Targeted Rule-Based Packet Capture and Export
description: Enable application-dump capture for a specific Palo Alto security rule, collect matching traffic, export the PCAP, and disable capture cleanly.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook captures traffic that matches a specific Palo Alto security rule using #set application dump on rule, then exports the resulting PCAP with #scp export filter-pcap or #tftp export filter-pcap.

  1. Enable capture: bind application dump to the target rule and adjust unknown-capture behavior if needed.
  2. Collect evidence: allow the firewall to capture packets for flows that hit the monitored rule.
  3. Export and clean up: move the PCAP off-box and disable the capture state to reduce performance impact.
metadata.commandsBlock:
# Step 1: Enable rule-based capture
> set application dump on rule <security-rule-name>

# Step 2: Optionally reduce unknown capture noise
> set application dump-unknown no
> configure
# set deviceconfig setting application dump-unknown off
# commit

# Step 3: Export packet capture
scp export filter-pcap from <capture> to <username@host:path>
tftp export filter-pcap from <capture> to <tftp-host>

# Step 4: Disable capture
> set application dump off
prerequisites: Known security rule name to monitor
Reachable SCP or TFTP destination if exporting externally
attackerGains: Exportable PCAP artifacts for offline analysis
detectionNotes: Packet capture is CPU-intensive and should be disabled promptly after collection
Restrict capture enablement and export rights to trusted administrators

steps.name: Prepare Capture Controls
steps.tooltip: This creates the operator-side control context for the packet-capture workflow.
steps.description: The operator opens a CLI session and prepares the rule-based capture commands.

steps.name: Bind Capture to Security Rule
steps.tooltip: The capture scope is narrowed to traffic that hits the monitored rule.
steps.description: Application dump is enabled for the target security rule so that matching packets will be captured.

steps.name: Tune Capture Engine Behavior
steps.tooltip: This step shapes how broadly the packet-capture engine records traffic.
steps.description: The operator disables unknown capture temporarily or persistently to reduce noisy packet collection.
steps.cli:
> set application dump-unknown no
> configure
# set deviceconfig setting application dump-unknown off
# commit

steps.name: Capture Rule-Matched Traffic
steps.tooltip: The firewall records traffic evidence only after the rule-scoped capture setting is active.
steps.description: Packets that traverse the monitored rule are written into the firewall's local filter-pcap store.

steps.name: Export Capture Off-Box
steps.tooltip: The PCAP leaves the firewall and becomes available for offline review.
steps.description: The firewall exports the selected capture stage to an SCP or TFTP destination for analysis or support escalation.
steps.cli:
scp export filter-pcap from <capture> to <username@host:path>
tftp export filter-pcap from <capture> to <tftp-host>

steps.name: Retrieve Evidence Artifact
steps.tooltip: This closes the capture loop by showing the PCAP in the operator's workflow.
steps.description: The exported PCAP is now available as an evidence artifact for the operator or an external analysis workflow.

steps.name: Disable Capture State
steps.tooltip: Leaving capture enabled longer than necessary can degrade firewall performance.
steps.description: The operator turns off application dump after sufficient evidence is collected.
